title: Lipidomic Analysis of Neuronal Lipid Droplets
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8l7xu","text":"This protocol provides details of extraction, identification, and quantification of lipids from purified lipid droplets using advanced LC-MS techniques, enabling detailed analysis of constituent lipids.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"h7vyf","text":"The protocol needs prior approval by the users' Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) or equivalent ethics committee.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare the LD samples for extraction by ensuring they are thawed On ice and homogenized.
Add MTBE to the LD samples along with spiked Avanti Splashmix (internal standards) to normalize lipid quantification.
Vortex the mixture thoroughly to ensure complete mixing.
Centrifuge the mixture to separate the organic phase.
Collect the organic phase carefully to avoid contamination from the aqueous phase.
Dry the collected organic phase under a gentle stream of nitrogen or in a vacuum concentrator.
Resuspend the dried lipid extract in 1-Butanol.
Vortex the sample to ensure uniform suspension.
Load 3 µL of the lipid mixture onto the LC-MS system.
Set up the Agilent Poroshell 120 EC-C18 column (2.7 μm, 2.1 × 100 mm) on the Agilent 1290 Infinity II LC system.
Prepare the following mobile phases: Mobile Phase A (60% Acetonitrile, 40% H₂O, 7.5 mM Ammonium Acetate), Mobile Phase B (90% IPA, 10% Acetonitrile, 7.5 mM Ammonium Acetate).

Use the following gradient for lipid separation: Start at 85% A (15% B) and decrease to 70% A over 00:02:00 . Further decrease to 52% A over 00:00:30 . Gradually reduce to 18% A over 00:05:00 . Reduce to 1% A within 00:01:00 and hold for 00:04:00 .
Restore gradient to 85% A and wash the column for 00:05:00 .
Perform the analysis on the Agilent Quadrupole Time-Of-Flight 6546 mass spectrometer for high-resolution detection of lipid species.
Process the LC-MS data using MS-DIAL software (version 4.7; PMID: 25938372).
Identify lipid species based on the mass spectra and retention times.

Quantify each lipid species using the moles of corresponding class-specific lipid standards present in the Splashmix. •Ensure the organic phase is free from aqueous contamination to avoid interference in LC-MS. •Handle MTBE and other organic solvents with care in a fume hood to prevent exposure. •The gradient setup must be precise to ensure reproducible lipid separation and identification.
creator: Mukesh Kumar
